name: agentdb-advanced-features
description: Master advanced AgentDB features including QUIC synchronization, multi-database management, custom distance metrics, hybrid search, and distributed systems integration. Use when building distributed AI systems, multi-agent coordination, or advanced vector search applications.
capabilities: Configure QUIC synchronization for sub-millisecond cross-node communication
Implement cosine, euclidean, and dot product distance metrics for vector similarity
Build hybrid searches combining vector similarity with metadata filters
Set up multi-database sharding for horizontal scaling across domains
Deploy production patterns with connection pooling and performance monitoring
author: ruvnet
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only SKILL.md with educational content about AgentDB features. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: markdown backticks flagged as shell execution, mathematical terms (cosine, dot) flagged as weak crypto, and generic network terms flagged as C2 keywords. No executable code exists. The pre-existing skill-report.json correctly assessed this as 'safe' with no security risks.
valueStatement: Build sophisticated distributed AI systems with sub-millisecond synchronization and advanced search capabilities. Learn to implement QUIC sync, custom distance metrics, and hybrid vector plus metadata search for production applications.
userTitle: Master Advanced AgentDB Vector Database Features
limitations: Requires AgentDB v1.0.7 or higher and Node.js 18 or higher
QUIC synchronization needs proper network configuration and firewall access
Advanced distributed features require understanding of distributed systems concepts

useCases.title: Distributed AI Agent Networks
useCases.description: Build multi-agent systems with synchronized memory across nodes for collaborative AI applications.

useCases.title: Hybrid Vector Search Systems
useCases.description: Create advanced search engines combining semantic similarity with business metadata filtering.

useCases.title: Production Vector Database Deployment
useCases.description: Deploy and manage scalable vector databases with monitoring, pooling, and synchronization.

promptTemplates.title: Basic QUIC Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up AgentDB with QUIC sync between two servers at 192.168.1.10 and 192.168.1.11 on port 4433

promptTemplates.title: Hybrid Search Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a hybrid search for products that finds similar items vectorially but filters by price range 10 to 100 dollars and category electronics

promptTemplates.title: Custom Distance Metric
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a custom distance function that weights the first half of vector dimensions two times more than the second half

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Database Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a sharding strategy for AgentDB that separates data by domain including users, products, and orders with automatic routing

bestPractices: Use connection pooling in production to avoid creating new adapters for each request
Implement proper error handling with exponential backoff for transient failures
Monitor query latency and set up alerts for performance degradation
antiPatterns: Do not expose QUIC ports directly to the internet without proper firewall rules
Avoid creating new database adapters for every operation - use pooling instead
Do not ignore dimension mismatch errors - they indicate data quality issues

faq.question: Is this compatible with standard AgentDB installations?
faq.answer: Yes, requires AgentDB v1.0.7 or higher via agentic-flow package. All features are backward compatible.

faq.question: What are the network requirements for QUIC sync?
faq.answer: UDP port 4433 must be open between peers. Works on local networks and VPNs.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with existing databases?
faq.answer: AgentDB is standalone but can complement existing databases. Use it for vector and pattern storage.

faq.question: How is data secured during synchronization?
faq.answer: QUIC uses TLS 1.3 encryption automatically. No additional configuration needed.

faq.question: What if QUIC sync fails between nodes?
faq.answer: Automatic retry with exponential backoff. Failed nodes catch up when reconnected.

faq.question: How does this compare to Pinecone or Weaviate?
faq.answer: AgentDB focuses on AI agent memory with reasoning capabilities, not just vector storage.
